channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Travis Kalanick launches a new company called Atoms focused on robotics
item.description: Kalanick's existing ghost kitchen company CloudKitchens will be rolled into Atoms, which also wants to get into mining and transport.

item.title: Title: Meta’s massive undersea cable project has been put on hold by regional hostilities – 2Africa Pearls project suffers another setback
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has forced Meta's 2Africa Red Sea and 'Pearls' sections to go on hold.

item.title: The FTC is mailing $47M in checks to renters—but many don’t realize they’re eligible
item.description: Invitation Homes allegedly charged undisclosed fees and withheld deposits. The FTC settlement will send payments to renters across the country. More than 444,000 people who rented homes with Invitation Homes will soon receive checks as part of a $47.2 million settlement stemming from a 2024 lawsuit filed by the Federal Trade Commission.

item.title: That Sure Is a Picture of a Knife, ‘Friday the 13th’ Spinoff ‘Crystal Lake’
item.description: At least Peacock remembered what day it is?

item.title: Best Dreo Spring Sale Deals: Air Fryer, Heater, Fans
item.description: Dreo’s spring sale offers a great deal on the Chefmaker combi cooker, plus discounts on WIRED’s favorite bathroom heater and tower fan.

item.title: ‘I have been Regina Georged’: Why the David protein bar lawsuit has social media cackling over ‘Mean Girls’ memes
item.description: Tiktokers are drawing comparisons between the company’s current controversy and an infamous movie scenario. A recent class-action lawsuit against David Protein, filed in January, alleges the company misrepresented the amount of calories and fat in its popular, healthy-branded bar, claiming that it had “way more” of both than customers were led to believe....

item.title: The IRS may owe you money from the pandemic years — but you have to claim it
item.description: Tax lawyers say Americans who paid IRS penalties or interest during the COVID-19 emergency could qualify for refunds after a federal court ruling shifted tax deadlines through July 2023. It’s possible that the IRS may owe you some money from the Covid era.

item.title: Live Nation Employees ‘Almost Feel Bad’ for Gouging Their Customers Who Are ‘So Stupid’
item.description: In Slack messages presented during the DOJ's antitrust case against the concert ticketing giant, employees gleefully describe how they're fleecing fans.

item.title: Google Pixel 10A review: Just buy the 9A
item.description: I'm not entirely sure why the Pixel 10A exists. Google hasn't upgraded the chipset, cameras, or battery in the new phone, and the tweaks it's made elsewhere are minimal at best. The flatter camera island is good, I guess! In one sense this isn't a big problem: The Pixel 9A is an excellent device, and […]

item.title: Earth’s Spin Is Slowing at a Pace Not Seen in Millions of Years—and You Can Guess Why
item.description: The new study described this "almost unprecedented rate of increase" in the length of an average day as a quantifiable consequence of Earth's rising oceans.

item.title: Elon Musk Is Dipping Into the Rejected Candidates Pile After Admitting xAI ‘Was Not Built Right’
item.description: We're all trying to find the guy who did this.

item.title: New Research on Muscle Loss Suggests Humans Will Really Suffer on Mars
item.description: Wanna colonize Mars? You may find these latest findings a bit discouraging.

item.title: How to watch The Madison season 1 — stream the Taylor Sheridan neo-Western from anywhere
item.description: Starring Michelle Pfeiffer and Kurt Russell as Clyburn family matriarch Stacy and patriarch Preston, here's how to watch The Madison season 1 online.

item.title: BlackRock will invest $100 million in trade work. It couldn’t come at a better time—here’s why
item.description: The funding also goes toward data centers, but the announcement comes at a time where skilled trade workers are more crucial than ever. BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, has said it would commit $100 million to training the next generation of skilled trades workers who can support a growing demand for new infrastructure.

item.title: Backbone’s versatile pro controller is nearly matching its best price to date
item.description: Mobile gaming has come a long way over the course of the last decade or so, but we all know that smartphones simply can’t match the visceral, tactile feel you get while playing with a dedicated controller. Luckily, Backbone makes some excellent mobile options — including last year’s Backbone Pro, which is on sale at […]

item.title: I Love Boosters review: Keke Palmer goes wild in Boots Rileys new sci-fi satire
item.description: Keke Palmer goes wild in Boots Riley's new sci-fi satire, "I Love Boosters." SXSW Review.

item.title: Adobe will pay $75 million to settle US cancellation fee lawsuit
item.description: Adobe says it will pay $75 million to resolve a lawsuit filed by the US government alleging that the creative software giant harmed consumers by making its subscriptions intentionally hard to cancel and concealing termination fees. The payment aims to resolve the complaint raised in June 2024, in which the US Justice Department accused Adobe […]

item.title: Chat Control: EU Parliament said no to Big Tech mass surveillance of your chats — but the battle for privacy isn't done
item.description: The EU Parliament's stance on untargeted scanning and encryption is clear. With the trialogue negotiations for the permanent law ongoing, will the Council and Commission be ready to follow suit?

item.title: U.S. economy expanded at just 0.7% in fourth quarter
item.description: Federal government spending and investment plunged at a 16.7% rate. The U.S. economy, hobbled by last fall’s 43-day government shutdown, advanced at an unexpectedly sluggish 0.7% annual rate from October through December, the Commerce Department reported Friday in a big downgrade of its initial estimate.

item.title: Dreame dévoile enfin sa stratégie smartphone : voici un nouvel OS mobile et 29 modèles
item.description: Dreame ne va plus seulement aspirer vos tapis. La marque chinoise vient d'annoncer une large gamme de smartphones à l'AWE de Shanghai avec un prototype de smartphone, le NEX LS1, dont le capteur photo se détache physiquement.

item.title: Le tout nouveau TV Xiaomi S Mini LED 55″ 4K avec un mode Game Boost 120 Hz n’échappe pas aux Ventes Flash de Printemps
item.description: À l'instar des smartphones, Xiaomi sort chaque année une nouvelle itération de ses téléviseurs S Mini LED : ici, la version 2026 de 55 pouces est en promo à 549 euros au lieu de 599 euros.

item.title: Sex kink is way more common than you think. This dating app’s free tool lets anybody explore those taboos
item.description: A new survey from Feeld shows that nearly half of people practice kink, but nontraditional sex still carries a strong stigma. Feeld markets itself as “the dating app for the curious.” For most of its users, that means curiosity about kink and casual sex—but its newest tool asks you to be curious about yourself.

item.title: Xiaomi Book Pro 14 : 50W de puissance dans 1 kg, mais une mauvaise nouvelle pour nous
item.description: Xiaomi sort enfin du silence sur le marché des PC portables premium. Le nouveau Book Pro 14 débarque avec les puces Intel Panther Lake gravées en 18A.

item.title: One of Our Favorite Large TVs Is $400 Off
item.description: The 85-inch Hisense U7 gets a big discount, with markdowns for smaller sizes too.

item.title: TSA Officers Miss Their First Full Paycheck as Fears About Long Airport Lines Get Real
item.description: Lines are expected to get much worse, and price hikes related to fuel-shortages are probably on the way.

item.title: You'll now have to fork out for an additional subscription if you want to watch 4K content on Prime Video
item.description: Amazon is raising the price of its ad-free Prime Video subscription and locking 4K UHD streaming behind this new tier. Starting April 10 for US customers, a rebranded Prime Video Ultra subscription will cost $5 per month, up from $3 per month. For that extra $2, you get a download capacity increase from 25 to 100, and you can now run five streams concurrently instead of three. Whether those "Ultra" upgrades are worth the $24 annual hike will probably depend on how many boxsets you like to plough through on a long flight, or how many devices are using your Prime Video account. The changes are m...

item.title: Loblaw confirms data breach - Canadian retail giant says 'basic customer information' affected
item.description: Hackers access "non-critical part of the IT network" at Loblaw, and stole names, emails, and phone numbers.

item.title: Test du Cosori Iconic Single : inox et céramique, une combinaison idéale pour une cuisine saine
item.description: Réputé pour ses appareils au design soigné, l'Américain Cosori monte en gamme avec l'Iconic Single. Arborant un look élégant et troquant le traditionnel revêtement en Téflon pour une cuve en céramique, ce modèle promet une cuisson aussi saine qu’efficace. Nous l'avons testé pendant plusieurs semaines pour vérifier si ses performances justifient son prix premium. ...

item.title: Full interview: Sonos CEO Tom Conrad explains why they built the new Sonos Play, how they're improving the app after its disaster, and what he thinks of Dolby Atmos FlexConnect and the state of music streaming services
item.description: AV Insider: Digging into the background of Sonos' return to releasing speakers, plus the threat to its soundbars from Dolby Atmos FlexConnect

item.title: Who needs a Chromebook? This £280 HP Windows 11 laptop deal is perfect for students and working from home in Amazon's Spring sale
item.description: This HP Windows 11 laptop is perfect for students and remote & hybrid workers and it's under £280 in Amazon's spring sale.
